rationale: The cited paper appears directly relevant to the citing paper’s core claim that an exogenous shock can change marketing-conduct effectiveness: both study changes in price/promotion effectiveness (elasticities/sensitivity) using quasi-experimental DID-style designs with controls for endogeneity. The citing note explicitly positions itself as extending marketing-conduct effectiveness research, and the cited note is a prominent empirical study on that exact construct in retail settings. Given this conceptual and methodological overlap, this is not an obvious incidental mention.
